title: Diffusion · Forward Noise & Reverse Denoise
tagline: Watch a 2D shape dissolve into Gaussian noise and reassemble itself
summary: DDPM corrupts data into noise over T steps and learns to reverse the corruption. Drag the timestep slider to see a 2D distribution (a spiral) dissolve into an isotropic Gaussian, then press 'reverse' to watch it reassemble. The intuition behind Stable Diffusion, one step at a time.
highlights: Slide timestep t from 0 to 1000; watch a 2D shape dissolve into noise.
Noise schedule toggle: linear vs cosine.
Reverse button animates denoising from pure noise back to the shape.
See closed-form forward: x_t = √(α̅_t) x_0 + √(1−α̅_t) ε.
Why diffusion beat GANs: stable training, diverse samples, strong likelihood.
